    # Returns whether the context has been compacted and no later model
    # response has cleared that state.
    # @return [Boolean]
    # @api private
    attr_accessor :compacted
    alias_method :compacted?, :compacted

    ##
    # Returns a guard, if configured.
    #
    # Guards are context-level supervisors for agentic execution. A guard can
    # inspect the runtime state and decide whether pending tool work should be
    # blocked before the context keeps looping.
    #
    # The built-in implementation is {LLM::LoopGuard LLM::LoopGuard}, which
    # detects repeated tool-call patterns and turns them into in-band
    # {LLM::GuardError LLM::GuardError} tool returns.
    #
    # @return [#call, nil]
    def guard
      return if @guard.nil? || @guard == false
      @guard = LLM::LoopGuard.new if @guard == true
      @guard = LLM::LoopGuard.new(@guard) if Hash === @guard
      @guard
    end

    ##
    # Sets a guard or guard config.
    #
    # Guards must implement `call(ctx)` and return either `nil` or a warning
    # string. Returning a warning tells the context to block pending tool work
    # with guarded tool errors instead of continuing the loop.
    #
    # @param [#call, Hash, Boolean, nil] guard
    # @return [#call, Hash, Boolean, nil]
    def guard=(guard)
      @guard = guard
    end

    ##
    # Returns a transformer, if configured.
    #
    # Transformers can rewrite outgoing prompts and params before a request is
    # sent to the provider.
    #
    # @return [#call, nil]
    def transformer
      @transformer
    end

    ##
    # Sets a transformer.
    #
    # Transformers must implement `call(ctx, prompt, params)` and return a
    # two-element array of `[prompt, params]`.
    #
    # @param [#call, nil] transformer
    # @return [#call, nil]
    def transformer=(transformer)
      @transformer = transformer
    end

    # Interact with the context via the chat completions API.
    # This method immediately sends a request to the LLM and returns the response.
    #
    # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    # @param params The params, including optional :role (defaults to :user), :stream, :tools, :schema etc.
    # @return [LLM::Response] Returns the LLM's response for this turn.
    # @example
    #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
    #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
    #   res = ctx.talk("Hello, what is your name?")
    #   puts res.messages[0].content
    def talk(prompt, params = {})
      @owner = @llm.request_owner
      compactor.compact!(prompt) if compactor.compact?(prompt)
      repair!(@messages, prompt)
      prompt, params, res = mode == :responses ? respond(prompt, params) : complete(prompt, params)
      self.compacted = false
      role = params[:role] || @llm.user_role
      role = @llm.tool_role if params[:role].nil? && [*prompt].grep(LLM::Function::Return).any?
...
    end

    ##
    # Builds in-band guarded returns when the guard blocks tool work.
    # @api private
    def guarded_returns(tools:)
      warning = guard&.call(self)
      return unless warning
      tools.map { guarded_return_for(_1, warning) }
    end

    ##
    # Rewrites a prompt and params through the configured transformer.
    # @api private
    def transform(prompt, params)
      return [prompt, params] unless transformer
      stream = params[:stream]
      stream.on_transform(self, transformer) if LLM::Stream === stream
      transformer.call(self, prompt, params)
    ensure
      stream.on_transform_finish(self, transformer) if LLM::Stream === stream
    end

    ##
    # Executes a turn through the Responses API.
    # @api private
    def respond(prompt, params)
      params = @params.merge(params)
      prompt, params = transform(prompt, params)
      bind!(params[:stream], params[:model], params[:tools])
      res_id = params[:store] == false ? nil : @messages.find(&:assistant?)&.response&.response_id
      params = params.merge(previous_response_id: res_id, input: @messages.to_a).compact
      [prompt, params, @llm.responses.create(prompt, params)]
    end

    ##
    # Executes a turn through the chat completions API.
    # @api private
    def complete(prompt, params)
      params = params.merge(messages: @messages.to_a)
      params = @params.merge(params)
      prompt, params = transform(prompt, params)
      bind!(params[:stream], params[:model], params[:tools])
      [prompt, params, @llm.complete(prompt, params)]
    end

    ##
    # Builds one guarded tool return for a blocked function call.
    # @api private
    def guarded_return_for(function, warning)
      LLM::Function::Return.new(function.id, function.name, {
        error: true,
        type: LLM::GuardError.name,
        message: warning
      })
    end

    ##
    # Emits tool return callbacks for directly waited function work.
    # @api private
    def emit_tool_returns(tools, returns)

  class Ollama < Provider
    require_relative "ollama/error_handler"
    require_relative "ollama/request_adapter"
    require_relative "ollama/response_adapter"
    require_relative "ollama/stream_parser"
    require_relative "ollama/models"

    include RequestAdapter

    HOST = "localhost"

    ##
    # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
    def initialize(**)
      super(host: HOST, port: 11434, ssl: false, **)
    end

    ##
    # @return [Symbol]
    #  Returns the provider's name
    def name
      :ollama
    end

    ##
    # Provides an embedding
    # @param input (see LLM::Provider#embed)

# frozen_string_literal: true

require "llm"
require "webmock/rspec"
require "vcr"
require "dotenv"

Dir[File.join(__dir__, "support/**/*.rb")].sort.each { require(_1) }
Dotenv.load

LLM.json = ENV.fetch("JSON_PARSER", "JSON")

RSpec.configure do |config|
  config.disable_monkey_patching!

  config.expect_with :rspec do |c|
    c.syntax = :expect
  end
end

VCR.configure do |config|
  config.cassette_library_dir = "spec/fixtures/cassettes"
  config.hook_into :webmock
  config.configure_rspec_metadata!

  ##
  # scrub
  config.filter_sensitive_data("TOKEN") { ENV["ANTHROPIC_SECRET"] }
  config.filter_sensitive_data("TOKEN") { ENV["GEMINI_SECRET"] }
  config.filter_sensitive_data("TOKEN") { ENV["OPENAI_SECRET"] }
  config.filter_sensitive_data("TOKEN") { ENV["DEEPSEEK_SECRET"] }
  config.filter_sensitive_data("TOKEN") { ENV["XAI_SECRET"] }
  config.filter_sensitive_data("TOKEN") { ENV["ZAI_SECRET"] }
  config.filter_sensitive_data("TOKEN") { ENV["AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"] }
  config.filter_sensitive_data("TOKEN") { ENV["AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"] }
  config.filter_sensitive_data("TOKEN") { ENV["AWS_SESSION_TOKEN"] }
  config.filter_sensitive_data("localhost") { ENV["OLLAMA_HOST"] }

  config.before_record do
    body = _1.response.body
    body.gsub! %r|#{Regexp.escape("https://oaidalleapiprodscus.blob.core.windows.net/")}[^"]+|,
               "https://openai.com/generated/image.png"
  end
end

I now have a complete picture. Let me write up all seven steps. --- # Step 1 — Orientation map **What it is:** `llm.rb` — a Ruby gem (`llm.gemspec`, `lib/llm.rb` is the root) providing a unified runtime over many external LLM providers, plus agents, tools, MCP, A2A, streaming, and tracing. **Layout:** - `lib/llm.rb` — top-level `LLM` module: requires, JSON adapter, model registry, and **factory methods** (`LLM.openai`, `LLM.anthropic`, `LLM.google`, `LLM.ollama`, `LLM.deepseek`, `LLM.xai`, `LLM.zai`, `LLM.bedrock`, `LLM.llamacpp`). Each lazy-requires its provider file then `Provider.new`. - `lib/llm/*.rb` — shared machinery: `provider.rb` (abstract base), `context.rb` (stateful chat boundary), `buffer.rb`, `transport.rb` + `transport/execution.rb` (HTTP execution), `response.rb`, `message.rb`, `contract.rb` + `contract/completion.rb` (response interface), `registry.rb` (model metadata from `data/*.json`), `error.rb` (typed errors), `stream.rb`, `eventstream.rb`. - `lib/llm/providers/.rb` + `lib/llm/providers//…` — one file per provider plus a per-provider subdirectory of components (`request_adapter`, `response_adapter`, `stream_parser`, `error_handler`, and capability interfaces like `models`, `files`, `images`, `audio`). - `data/.json` — model registry (pricing/limits/modalities). Present for anthropic, bedrock, deepseek, google, openai, xai, zai. (ollama/llamacpp ship none — registry is optional.) - `spec//…` — per-provider specs driven by shared examples in `spec/support/shared_examples/`, VCR cassettes in `spec/fixtures/cassettes//`, config in `spec/setup.rb`. **How a caller obtains a provider:** `LLM.(**)` factory in `lib/llm.rb:111-175` → returns an `LLM::` instance (a `LLM::Provider` subclass). **Provider families:** "native" providers subclass `LLM::Provider` directly (OpenAI `lib/llm/providers/openai.rb:17`, Anthropic `:16`, Google, Ollama, Bedrock). "Thin" providers subclass **OpenAI** and disable unsupported pieces (DeepSeek `lib/llm/providers/deepseek.rb:21`, XAI `xai.rb:16`, ZAI `zai.rb:15`, LlamaCpp `llamacpp.rb:20`). --- # Step 2 — Trace of a chat call (entry → request → response) Order of execution for `LLM.openai(key:).chat(...)` / `ctx.talk(...)`: 1. **Obtain provider:** `LLM.openai(**)` — `lib/llm.rb:157-160` → `LLM::OpenAI.new` (`lib/llm/providers/openai.rb:32`). 2. **Entry:** `LLM::Provider#chat` (`lib/llm/provider.rb:99-102`) builds `LLM::Context.new(self, params).talk(...)`. (`#complete` itself is abstract at `provider.rb:90`.) 3. **Context turn:** `LLM::Context#talk` (`lib/llm/context.rb:545`) → dispatches to `#complete` (`context.rb:561-566`), which merges history (`messages: @messages.to_a`) and calls `@llm.complete(prompt, params)` at `context.rb:563`. 4. **Provider#complete:** `LLM::OpenAI#complete` (`lib/llm/providers/openai.rb:71-80`): - `normalize_complete_params` (`openai.rb:202`) — defaults, schema/tools adaptation, stream flag. - `build_complete_request` (`openai.rb:212`) — calls `adapt(messages, mode: :complete)` then builds `LLM::Transport::Request.post(completions_path, headers)`. 5. **Request adapter (build body):** `RequestAdapter#adapt` (`openai/request_adapter.rb:20`) → per-message `RequestAdapter::Completion#adapt` (`openai/request_adapter/completion.rb:16`). `headers` at `openai.rb:189`, path at `openai.rb:184`. 6. **Execute (HTTP):** `execute(...)` (`lib/llm/transport/execution.rb:36`) → `transport.request` → `handle_response` (`execution.rb:53`): on success `parse_response` (`execution.rb:64`, JSON→`LLM::Object`); on failure `error_handler.new(...).raise_error!`. 7. **Response adapter (parse):** back in `complete`, `ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)` (`openai/response_adapter.rb:24` → selects `ResponseAdapter::Completion`, `openai/response_adapter/completion.rb`). This extends the `LLM::Response` with `messages`, `usage`, `model`, token counts — fulfilling `LLM::Contract::Completion`. 8. **Return:** `LLM::Response` flows back through `context.rb:563` into the buffer; `talk` appends assistant messages. Streaming variant: when `params[:stream]` is set, `execute` wraps the stream with `stream_parser` (`openai/stream_parser.rb`) — see Step 5. --- # Step 3 — Shared base contract & how providers customize it **Base:** `LLM::Provider` (`lib/llm/provider.rb:8`). Methods a provider may override (those raising `NotImplementedError` are required if the capability is offered): | Contract method | Base location | Required? | |---|---|---| | `name` | `provider.rb:54` | yes (every provider) | | `complete` | `provider.rb:90` | yes (chat) | | `chat` | `provider.rb:99` | inherited (builds Context) — rarely overridden | | `respond` | `provider.rb:109` | inherited | | `embed` | `provider.rb:64` | if embeddings supported | | `assistant_role` | `provider.rb:166` | yes | | `default_model` | `provider.rb:173` | yes | | `headers` (private) | `provider.rb:328` | yes | | `error_handler` (private) | `provider.rb:337` | yes | | `stream_parser` (private) | `provider.rb:349` | yes (for streaming) | | `stream_decoder` (private) | `provider.rb:356` | default `LLM::Transport::StreamDecoder`; Bedrock overrides | | `event_handler` (private) | `provider.rb:343` | default `LLM::EventHandler` | | Capability accessors `responses`/`images`/`audio`/`files`/`models`/`moderations`/`vector_stores` | `provider.rb:121-159` | each raises `NotImplementedError` unless overridden | | `web_search`, `server_tools` | `provider.rb:223`, `:194` | optional | | role accessors `user_role`/`system_role`/`developer_role`/`tool_role` | `provider.rb:236-256` | defaults; Anthropic overrides `tool_role` | | `schema` | `provider.rb:162` | default `LLM::Schema` | **Native providers (subclass `Provider`):** - **OpenAI** (`openai.rb:17`) — fullest: `embed`, `complete`, `responses`, `images`, `audio`, `files`, `models`, `moderations`, `vector_stores`, `web_search`, `server_tools`; `assistant_role`="assistant", `default_model`="gpt-4.1", base_path "/v1". - **Anthropic** (`anthropic.rb:16`) — `complete`, `models`, `files`, `web_search`, `server_tools`; **no** `embed`; overrides `tool_role` → `:user` (`anthropic.rb:91`); `max_tokens: 1024` default param (`anthropic.rb:151`); custom headers (`x-api-key`, `anthropic-version`). No `embed`/`images`/`audio`/`responses`. - **Google** (`google.rb`) — `embed` (`:56`), `complete` (`:77`), plus `images`/`audio`/`files`/`models` (per its subdir); distinct `headers`/`stream_parser`/`error_handler` (`:180-192`). - **Ollama** (`ollama.rb:18`) — `embed` (`:49`), `complete` (`:69`), `models` (`:??`); `assistant_role`="assistant", `default_model`="qwen3:latest"; OpenAI-style headers but its own adapters. - **Bedrock** (`bedrock.rb:33`) — AWS SigV4 auth (no `key:`; takes `access_key_id`/`secret_access_key`/`region`); overrides `stream_decoder` (binary AWS event stream); own `signature.rb`, `stream_decoder.rb`. **Thin providers (subclass `OpenAI`)** — reuse OpenAI's `complete`/`embed`/adapters/stream_parser/error_handler, override `name`/`default_model`/host, and **disable** unsupported capabilities by re-raising `NotImplementedError`: - **DeepSeek** (`deepseek.rb:21`) — host `api.deepseek.com`; includes its **own** `DeepSeek::RequestAdapter` (`deepseek.rb:23-24`) replacing OpenAI's; disables `files`/`images`/`audio`/`moderations`/`responses`/`vector_stores` (`:39-72`); `default_model`="deepseek-v4-flash". - **XAI** (`xai.rb:16`) — host `api.x.ai`; **overrides `images`** with `LLM::XAI::Images` (`xai.rb:46`); disables `files`/`audio`/`moderations`/`responses`/`vector_stores`; `default_model`="grok-4-0709". - **ZAI** (`zai.rb:15`) — host `api.z.ai`; overrides private `completions_path` → `/api/paas/v4/chat/completions` (`zai.rb:79`); disables everything except chat; `default_model`="glm-4.5-flash". **Leanest template** (no own components). - **LlamaCpp** (`llamacpp.rb:20`) — `host: localhost, port: 8080, ssl: false`; disables all capability APIs; `default_model`="qwen3". --- # Step 4 — Per-provider component families Each native provider owns a parallel set of components. Tie-together = the `require_relative` + `include`/accessor lines in the provider file. **RequestAdapter** (builds outgoing body): - OpenAI: `openai/request_adapter.rb` + `request_adapter/completion.rb` (+ `respond.rb`, `moderation.rb`); `include RequestAdapter` at `openai.rb:28`. - Anthropic: `anthropic/request_adapter.rb` + `request_adapter/completion.rb`; `include` at `anthropic.rb:23`. - Google: `google/request_adapter.rb` + `request_adapter/completion.rb`. - Ollama: `ollama/request_adapter.rb` + `request_adapter/completion.rb`; `include` at `ollama.rb:25`. - Bedrock: `bedrock/request_adapter.rb` + `request_adapter/completion.rb`. - DeepSeek: own `deepseek/request_adapter.rb` + `request_adapter/completion.rb` (overrides OpenAI's). XAI/ZAI/LlamaCpp reuse OpenAI's. **ResponseAdapter** (parses response → library shape; dispatched by `type:`): - OpenAI: `openai/response_adapter.rb` (selector at `:24-50`) + subfiles `completion`, `embedding`, `audio`, `image`, `file`, `models`, `moderations`, `responds`, `web_search`, `enumerable`. - Anthropic: `anthropic/response_adapter.rb` + `completion`, `enumerable`, `file`, `models`, `web_search`. - Google: `google/response_adapter.rb` + `completion`, `embedding`, `file`, `files`, `image`, `models`, `web_search`. - Ollama: `ollama/response_adapter.rb` + `completion`, `embedding`, `models`. - Bedrock: `bedrock/response_adapter.rb` + `completion`, `models`. - Thin providers reuse OpenAI's ResponseAdapter. **StreamParser** (incremental parse): `openai/stream_parser.rb` (+ `responses/stream_parser.rb`), `anthropic/stream_parser.rb`, `google/stream_parser.rb`, `ollama/stream_parser.rb`, `bedrock/stream_parser.rb` (+ `bedrock/stream_decoder.rb`). Wired via private `stream_parser` accessor (e.g. `openai.rb:194`). **ErrorHandler**: `openai/error_handler.rb`, `anthropic/…`, `google/…`, `ollama/…`, `bedrock/…`. Wired via private `error_handler` accessor (e.g. `openai.rb:198`). **Capability components** (own subclasses, exposed via accessor): OpenAI `models.rb`/`files.rb`/`images.rb`/`audio.rb`/`moderations.rb`/`vector_stores.rb`/`responses.rb`; Anthropic `models.rb`/`files.rb`; Google `models.rb`/`files.rb`/`images.rb`/`audio.rb`; Ollama `models.rb`; Bedrock `models.rb`/`signature.rb`; XAI `images.rb`. --- # Step 5 — Streaming & error paths **Streaming** — `execute` (`transport/execution.rb:36`) wraps the stream: `stream &&= LLM::Object.from(streamer:, parser: stream_parser, decoder: stream_decoder)`. Each provider's `StreamParser` exposes `attr_reader :body` and a `parse!(chunk)` that merges deltas: - OpenAI `openai/stream_parser.rb:6` — `parse!` (`:42`) merges `choices[].delta` (content/reasoning_content/tool_calls), emits via `on_content`/`on_reasoning_content`/`on_tool_call` (`:188-220`). Reused by all thin providers. - Anthropic `anthropic/stream_parser.rb:6` — `parse!` at `:27` (event-block deltas). - Google `google/stream_parser.rb:6` — `parse!` at `:28`. - Ollama `ollama/stream_parser.rb:6` — `parse!` at `:23`. - Bedrock `bedrock/stream_parser.rb:19` — `parse!(payload, event_type:)` at `:42`, fed by binary `bedrock/stream_decoder.rb` (overrides base `stream_decoder`).
